title: Grammitis coredrosora
taxonomy: Grammitis coredrosora is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This classification places it within the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns and their allies. Grammitis coredrosora is further categorized within the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. At the phylum level, it is classified as Pteridophyta, and at the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae. The specific epithet 'coredrosora' is a name given to this particular species within the Grammitis genus.
uses: Grammitis coredrosora is reportedly used in traditional medicine in some regions, where it is believed to have various health benefits. Its leaves are said to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help treat wounds and reduce swelling. Additionally, it is sometimes used to treat fever and digestive issues. Some people also use it as a treatment for respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Grammitis coredrosora for medicinal purposes.
